skill.name: pdf
skill.description: PDF processing toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating PDFs, merging and splitting documents, converting pages to images, and filling PDF forms with Python and command-line tools.
skill.summary: Process, inspect, convert, and fill PDF documents with local scripts and PDF libraries.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many potential issues, including high-risk keyword matches. Manual review found most high and network findings are false positives from markdown examples, PDF terminology, tests, and documentation links. Confirmed risk is medium because the skill runs local tools and writes user-specified output files, but no malicious intent or network exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally instructs agents to run local Python scripts and command-line PDF tools. This is useful for PDF work, but it can execute tools against untrusted PDFs and user-provided paths, so users should review commands before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command lines are explicit and central to the skill workflow. I found no shell interpolation or hidden execution path, so the concern is controlled local execution rather than malicious command injection.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Writes
medium_findings.description: Helper scripts write JSON, PNG, and PDF output files to paths supplied by the user or agent. This can overwrite files if the output path is chosen poorly, but there is no evidence of stealthy writes or persistence.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly open or save output paths provided through arguments. The behavior matches the documented PDF workflow and does not include hidden destinations.

low_findings.title: Runtime Library Monkeypatch
low_findings.description: One helper patches a pypdf method at runtime to handle selection list fields. The patch is local to the script process and appears to be a compatibility workaround, but it changes library behavior during execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The monkeypatch is explicit and scoped to a known pypdf issue. It is not a security bypass, but runtime patching is a maintenance and behavior risk.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Non-Runtime References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to license terms and documentation comments. I found no runtime HTTP client or data upload behavior in the inspected helper scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are legal or issue-reference URLs, not network calls. No evidence of exfiltration or external service use was found.

low_findings.title: High-Risk Keyword Matches Are Benign PDF Examples
low_findings.description: The static high-severity weak crypto and C2 keyword alerts align with PDF encryption examples, command examples, test fixture text, or ordinary document-processing vocabulary. No evidence found for malware control channels, credential theft, or malicious cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed PDF password protection, merge examples, and bounding-box tests at representative flagged locations. These do not demonstrate malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill intentionally instructs agents to run local Python scripts and command-line PDF tools. This is useful for PDF work, but it can execute tools against untrusted PDFs and user-provided paths, so users should review commands before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command lines are explicit and central to the skill workflow. I found no shell interpolation or hidden execution path, so the concern is controlled local execution rather than malicious command injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Local File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: Helper scripts write JSON, PNG, and PDF output files to paths supplied by the user or agent. This can overwrite files if the output path is chosen poorly, but there is no evidence of stealthy writes or persistence.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly open or save output paths provided through arguments. The behavior matches the documented PDF workflow and does not include hidden destinations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runtime Library Monkeypatch
dangerous_patterns.description: One helper patches a pypdf method at runtime to handle selection list fields. The patch is local to the script process and appears to be a compatibility workaround, but it changes library behavior during execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The monkeypatch is explicit and scoped to a known pypdf issue. It is not a security bypass, but runtime patching is a maintenance and behavior risk.

content.value_statement: PDF work often requires many small tools for text, tables, images, and forms. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical workflows for local PDF processing.
actual_capabilities: Extract text, metadata, tables, and images from PDF documents.
Create, merge, split, rotate, crop, watermark, and encrypt PDFs.
Convert PDF pages into PNG images for visual inspection and OCR workflows.
Detect fillable form fields and generate field information for form completion.
Fill fillable PDFs or add annotation text to non-fillable PDF forms.
Validate form bounding boxes before writing annotations to a final PDF.
limitations: Requires local Python packages and command-line tools to be installed.
Does not provide a graphical PDF editor or manual drag-and-drop interface.
Scanned documents need OCR tools before reliable text extraction is possible.
Output quality depends on PDF structure, fonts, form fields, and viewer behavior.

use_cases.title: Extract contract data
use_cases.description: Pull text, tables, and metadata from received PDFs for review or downstream analysis.

use_cases.title: Prepare office document packets
use_cases.description: Merge, split, rotate, watermark, and password-protect PDF files for routine document handling.

use_cases.title: Complete structured PDF forms
use_cases.description: Identify fields, validate placements, and generate filled PDFs from known user data.

prompt_templates.title: Extract text from a PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to extract readable text from this PDF. Tell me which tool you will use and save the extracted text as a separate file.

prompt_templates.title: Merge and organize files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to merge these PDFs in the order I provide. Rotate any sideways pages and create one final document.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a PDF form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF form workflow to determine whether this PDF has fillable fields. Then fill it with the values I provide and validate the result.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a batch of PDFs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PDF skill to inspect a folder of PDFs. Extract metadata, page counts, text availability, and any tables, then summarize processing issues.

output_examples.input: A three-page invoice PDF with one embedded table.
output_examples.output: A text summary of each page, one extracted table saved separately, and notes about any missing or low-confidence cells.

output_examples.input: A non-fillable registration form and user-provided answers.
output_examples.output: A filled PDF with annotation text placed in validated fields, plus a short list of fields that required manual review.

output_examples.input: Five PDFs that must become one packet.
output_examples.output: One merged PDF in the requested order, with rotated pages corrected and a brief processing log.

best_practices: Work on copies of original PDFs and write outputs to a dedicated folder.
Review every command before running it, especially when paths or passwords are included.
Visually inspect validation images before writing annotations to non-fillable forms.
anti_patterns: Do not run PDF tools on untrusted files without sandboxing or review.
Do not overwrite original PDFs unless the user explicitly asks for replacement.
Do not assume OCR text, form fields, or bounding boxes are accurate without validation.

faq.question: Can this skill fill PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports fillable fields and annotation-based filling for non-fillable forms.

faq.question: Does this skill need internet access?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed workflows operate on local files and local tools.

faq.question: Can it process scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, but scanned PDFs need OCR tools before text extraction is reliable.

faq.question: Which tools does it use?
faq.answer: It references pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab, pdf2image, pytesseract, qpdf, pdftk, and related PDF utilities.

faq.question: Will it preserve original formatting?
faq.answer: Usually for merge, split, rotate, and form updates. Annotation and OCR workflows may need visual review.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning. The skill uses local scripts and file writes, but no malicious behavior was found.
